game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: **Kiwanuka:** Players guide a group of small, glowing "Kiwanuka" citizens across a 2D environment by tapping and dragging a glowing staff to direct their collective movement, with the entire group following the staff's path. The core mechanic involves tapping a citizen to select them, then tapping and holding to instruct them to form a vertical tower, with up to 5 citizens stacking on top of each other, consuming a portion of the group's total available citizens. Once a tower is built, players can tap and swipe to knock it over in a specific direction, creating a bridge or ramp that allows the remaining citizens to traverse gaps or reach higher ledges. Successful level completion requires guiding a minimum threshold of citizens (e.g., 70% of the starting group) to a designated exit portal, with failure occurring if too many citizens fall off the map or the exit becomes unreachable. Progression through the game's 60+ levels introduces new environmental hazards, puzzle elements, and citizen abilities, demanding precise timing and strategic tower placement to overcome increasingly complex challenges.
game_info.description: Guide your glowing Kiwanuka citizens across challenging platforms to reach the exit portal! Stack citizens into towers by selecting them with Z, then topple the towers with arrow keys to create bridges across gaps. You must guide at least 70% of your citizens safely to the exit to complete each level. Watch out for deadly falls and unreachable areas!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move the guide staff to direct citizens / Topple tower in direction
Z: Select/deselect citizen to build tower
Space: Release selected citizen from tower
Shift: Speed up citizen movement
Enter: Start game
Esc: Pause/unpause
R: Restart to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and citizen following test. Validates that citizens follow the staff and respond to directional inputs correctly. Tests fundamental game mechanics without complex tower building.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move the staff around the level using arrow keys, ensuring citizens follow. Navigate towards the exit portal without building towers. Test basic pathfinding and citizen grouping behavior.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Citizens should follow the staff smoothly and consistently. Movement should be responsive to arrow key inputs. Citizens should not get stuck or behave erratically. Test passes when basic movement mechanics work correctly.

TEST_1.expected: Citizens should follow the staff smoothly and consistently. Movement should be responsive to arrow key inputs. Citizens should not get stuck or behave erratically. Test passes when basic movement mechanics work correctly.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition test using optimal tower strategy. Tests complete gameplay loop including tower building, toppling, and successful level completion with 70%+ citizens reaching the exit.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Analyze level layout to identify gaps requiring bridges. Build towers at strategic locations using Z key. Topple towers in optimal directions to create bridges. Guide remaining citizens across bridges to exit portal efficiently.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully guide 70% or more citizens to the exit portal. Achieve GAME_OVER_WIN state. Validate that tower mechanics, bridge creation, and citizen pathfinding work correctly together to complete the level.
TEST_2.description: Win condition test using optimal tower strategy. Tests complete gameplay loop including tower building, toppling, and successful level completion with 70%+ citizens reaching the exit.

TEST_2.expected: Successfully guide 70% or more citizens to the exit portal. Achieve GAME_OVER_WIN state. Validate that tower mechanics, bridge creation, and citizen pathfinding work correctly together to complete the level.

TEST_3.test_description: Tower building mechanics test. Validates that citizens can be selected, stacked vertically, and that the tower height limit (5 citizens) is enforced correctly.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Select multiple citizens using Z key and verify they stack into a tower. Test that towers consume citizens from the group. Verify maximum height constraint. Test tower stability before toppling.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Citizens should stack vertically when selected. Tower should maintain structural integrity. Maximum 5 citizens per tower. Tower building should correctly reduce available citizen count.
TEST_3.description: Tower building mechanics test. Validates that citizens can be selected, stacked vertically, and that the tower height limit (5 citizens) is enforced correctly.
TEST_3.strategy: Select multiple citizens using Z key and verify they stack into a tower. Test that towers consume citizens from the group. Verify maximum height constraint. Test tower stability before toppling.
TEST_3.expected: Citizens should stack vertically when selected. Tower should maintain structural integrity. Maximum 5 citizens per tower. Tower building should correctly reduce available citizen count.

TEST_4.test_description: Bridge creation through tower toppling. Tests that towers can be knocked over in specific directions to create functional bridges that citizens can walk across.
TEST_4.strategy_description: Build a tower near a gap, then use arrow keys to topple it in the direction needed to span the gap. Verify that fallen citizens form a bridge. Guide other citizens across the bridge.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Towers should topple smoothly in the specified direction. Fallen citizens should form traversable bridges. Other citizens should be able to walk across bridges without falling through.
